name: Luteolin
description: Luteolin is a dietary flavone studied for anti-inflammatory and redox-active pharmacology. Evidence includes mechanistic studies and translational safety reviews with potential drug-interaction liability.
effects: In human and translational reviews, luteolin is discussed for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant pathway modulation.
Mechanistic literature reports modulation of CYP enzymes and transporters, which may alter co-administered drug exposure.
contraindications: Use caution with narrow-therapeutic-index drugs because CYP/transporter modulation may change exposure.
Evidence for efficacy is mixed and largely translational; avoid treating as a substitute for standard care.
interactions: Potential interaction risk with CYP-metabolized medicines and transporter substrates; monitor for altered drug response.

sources.title: Drug interaction and safety implications of luteolin supplementation: translational review.
Luteolin modulation of CYP and transporter pathways: integrated mechanistic in vitro and in vivo analysis.
